title: Best Veggie Burger
image_alt: Veggie burger with mushroom and walnut patty on a bun with toppings

items.name: extra-virgin olive oil, plus more for drizzling
mushrooms (shiitake and portobello), stemmed and diced
sea salt and freshly ground black pepper

steps: Heat olive oil in a medium skillet over medium heat. Add shallots and sauté until soft, about 1 minute. Add mushrooms with a pinch of salt and cook until soft and browned, 6–9 minutes.
Stir in tamari, balsamic vinegar, and mirin. Reduce heat, then add garlic, smoked paprika, and sriracha. Stir to combine and let cool slightly.
In a food processor, combine the mushroom mixture, walnuts, flaxseed, brown rice, and ½ cup of the panko. Pulse until combined but with some texture remaining — don't over-process.
Transfer mixture to a bowl and fold in the remaining ½ cup panko.
Form into 8 patties, place on a plate, and refrigerate for at least 1 hour (or up to 1 day ahead).
To grill: Preheat grill to medium-high. Brush patties with olive oil, spray the grill with cooking spray, and cook 7 minutes per side until charred and cooked through.
To cook on the stovetop: Heat a cast-iron skillet over medium heat with a drizzle of oil. Cook patties 5–6 minutes per side until charred.
Brush cooked patties with vegan Worcestershire sauce and serve on buns with desired toppings.
